from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural‑language sentence from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Predicate mappings that create natural language
    templates = {
        "numberOfMembers": "{subj} has {obj} members",
        "season": "{subj} competed in the {obj} season",
        "league": "{subj} plays in the {obj} league",
        "manager": "{subj} is managed by {obj}",
        "ground": "{subj} plays at {obj}",
        "fullName": "{subj}'s full name is {obj}",
        "chairman": "{subj}'s chairman is {obj}",
        "location": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "owner": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
        "nickname": "{subj} is nicknamed {obj}",
        "chairmanTitle": "{subj}'s chairman holds the title of {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
        "leader": "{subj}'s leader is {obj}",
        "part": "{subj} includes the area {obj}",
        "mayor": "{subj}'s mayor is {obj}",
        "champions": "{obj} are the champions of {subj}",
        "country": "{subj} is in {obj}",
        "club": "{subj} plays for {obj}",
        "capital": "{subj}'s capital is {obj}",
        "currency": "{subj} uses {obj} as currency",
        "demonym": "People from {subj} are called {obj}",
        "language": "{subj} speaks {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "{subj}'s leader belongs to {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "{subj}'s official language is {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "{subj} was born in {obj}",
        "youthclub": "{subj} started their career at {obj}",
        "operator": "{subj} is operated by {obj}",
        "tenant": "{subj} is the home of {obj}",
        "city": "{subj} is in {obj}",
        "state": "{subj} is in the state of {obj}",
    }

    # Get the main subject (most common subject in triples)
    subject_counts = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_counts[t.subject] = subject_counts.get(t.subject, 0) + 1
    main_subject = max(subject_counts.keys(), key=lambda x: subject_counts[x])

    # Build phrases for all triples
    phrases = []
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "{subj} {pred} {obj}")
        phrase = tmpl.format(subj=t.subject, obj=t.object)
        phrases.append((phrase, t.subject))

    # Separate phrases by main subject vs others
    main_phrases = [p[0] for p in phrases if p[1] == main_subject]
    other_phrases = [p[0] for p in phrases if p[1] != main_subject]

    # Build the sentence
    if len(main_phrases) == 1 and len(other_phrases) == 0:
        # Single triple case
        return main_phrases[0] + "."
    elif len(main_phrases) == 0:
        # Fallback to first triple
        return phrases[0][0] + "."
    else:
        # Combine all main subject phrases
        if len(main_phrases) == 1:
            sentence = main_phrases[0]
        else:
            # Join multiple main phrases with "and"
            sentence = ", ".join(main_phrases[:-1]) + " and " + main_phrases[-1]

        # Add other subject phrases as relative clauses
        for other in other_phrases:
            # Find connection point (object that matches another subject)
            # Convert to relative clause
            if "is" in other:
                other = other.replace("is ", "which is ", 1)
            elif "plays" in other:
                other = other.replace("plays", "which plays", 1)
            else:
                other = f"which {other.lower()}"
            sentence += f", {other}"

        return sentence + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural‑language sentence from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build a graph of subject -> object relationships for chaining
    subj_to_obj = {}
    obj_to_subj = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj_to_obj[t.subject] = (t.predicate, t.object)
        if t.object not in obj_to_subj:
            obj_to_subj[t.object] = []
        obj_to_subj[t.object].append((t.subject, t.predicate))

    # Better predicate mappings that create natural language
    templates = {
        "numberOfMembers": "{subj} has {obj} members",
        "season": "{subj} competed in the {obj} season",
        "league": "{subj} plays in the {obj} league",
        "manager": "{subj} is managed by {obj}",
        "ground": "{subj} plays at {obj}",
        "fullName": "{subj}'s full name is {obj}",
        "chairman": "{subj}'s chairman is {obj}",
        "location": "{subj} is located in {obj}",
        "owner": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
        "nickname": "{subj} is nicknamed {obj}",
        "chairmanTitle": "{subj}'s chairman holds the title of {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
        "leader": "{subj}'s leader is {obj}",
        "part": "{subj} includes the area {obj}",
        "mayor": "{subj}'s mayor is {obj}",
        "champions": "{obj} are the champions of {subj}",
        "country": "{subj} is in {obj}",
        "club": "{subj} plays for {obj}",
        "capital": "{subj}'s capital is {obj}",
        "currency": "{subj} uses {obj} as currency",
        "demonym": "People from {subj} are called {obj}",
        "language": "{subj} speaks {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "{subj}'s leader belongs to {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "{subj}'s official language is {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "{subj} was born in {obj}",
        "youthclub": "{subj} started their career at {obj}",
        "operator": "{subj} is operated by {obj}",
        "tenant": "{subj} is the home of {obj}",
        "city": "{subj} is in {obj}",
        "state": "{subj} is in the state of {obj}",
    }

    # Build phrases for each triple
    phrase_map = {}
    triple_list = []
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "{subj} {pred} {obj}")
        phrase = tmpl.format(subj=t.subject, obj=t.object)
        phrase_map[t.subject] = phrase
        triple_list.append((t.subject, t.predicate, t.object))

    # Find a starting subject (one that doesn't appear as an object in other triples)
    all_subjects = set(t.subject for t in triples)
    all_objects = set(t.object for t in triples)
    starting_subjects = all_subjects - all_objects
    if not starting_subjects:
        starting_subjects = all_subjects

    # Build sentence by chaining related information with proper relative clauses
    if len(triples) == 1:
        t = triples[0]
        return templates.get(t.predicate, f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}") + "."

    # Build a connected sentence using relative clauses
    sentence_parts = []
    used_triples = set()
    current_subject = next(iter(starting_subjects)) if starting_subjects else triples[0].subject

    # Start with the main subject's information
    main_phrase = phrase_map.get(current_subject, f"{current_subject} {subj_to_obj[current_subject][0]} {subj_to_obj[current_subject][1]}")
    sentence_parts.append(main_phrase)
    used_triples.add(current_subject)

    # Find and chain related triples
    for _ in range(len(triples)):
        found_connection = False
        for t in triples:
            if t.subject in used_triples:
                continue
            # Check if this triple's object matches a used subject
            if t.object in used_triples:
                phrase = phrase_map.get(t.subject, f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}")
                # Convert to relative clause
                if "is" in phrase:
                    relative_phrase = phrase.replace("is ", "which is ", 1)
                elif "plays" in phrase:
                    relative_phrase = phrase.replace("plays", "which plays", 1)
                else:
                    relative_phrase = f"which {phrase.lower()}"
                sentence_parts.append(relative_phrase)
                used_triples.add(t.subject)
                found_connection = True
                break
            # Check if this triple's subject matches a used object
            elif t.subject in all_objects and t.subject in used_triples:
                phrase = phrase_map.get(t.subject, f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}")
                relative_phrase = phrase.replace("is ", "where is ", 1) if "is" in phrase else f", where {phrase.lower()}"
                sentence_parts.append(relative_phrase)
                used_triples.add(t.subject)
                found_connection = True
                break

        if not found_connection:
            break

    # Join with appropriate connectors
    if len(sentence_parts) == 1:
        sentence = sentence_parts[0] + "."
    else:
        # Use relative clauses to create a flowing sentence
        sentence = sentence_parts[0]
        for i, part in enumerate(sentence_parts[1:], 1):
            if i == 1:
                sentence += f", {part}"
            elif i == len(sentence_parts) - 1:
                sentence += f", and {part}."
            else:
                sentence += f", {part}"

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

        return ""

    # Predicate mappings that create natural language

    # Build the sentence
    if len(main_phrases) == 1 and len(other_phrases) == 0:
        # Single triple case
        return main_phrases[0] + "."
    elif len(main_phrases) == 0:
        # Fallback to first triple
        return phrases[0][0] + "."
    else:
        # Combine all main subject phrases
        if len(main_phrases) == 1:
            sentence = main_phrases[0]
        else:
            # Join multiple main phrases with "and"
            sentence = ", ".join(main_phrases[:-1]) + " and " + main_phrases[-1]
        
        # Add other subject phrases as relative clauses
        for other in other_phrases:
            # Find connection point (object that matches another subject)
            # Convert to relative clause
            if "is" in other:
                other = other.replace("is ", "which is ", 1)
            elif "plays" in other:
                other = other.replace("plays", "which plays", 1)
            else:
                other = f"which {other.lower()}"
            sentence += f", {other}"
        
        return sentence + "."
